name: sprint18_model_loaded_roundtrip
methodology: Sprint-18 model-loaded @hardware tiers, agent-run on the dev box under the #629 automate-first reframe (the agent ran every tier; no operator terminal time). Each tier is a single cold run (n=1); tiers run one-at-a-time in separate pytest processes (one 14B in Arc 140V VRAM at a time, no co-residency). C1: full gateway->AO round-trip in PRODUCTION posture (dev_mode=False, per-boot-minted mTLS via provision_per_boot_certs, require_signed_manifest=true with detached manifest.json.sig verified, real PGOV output validation). C2: model-loaded IPC-routing regression lock (gateway->AO port resolution, no 'Unsupported message type' misroute under real load). C3: real bge-small SemanticRouter.classify() + a real AO turn driven separately (the router is NOT wired into the AO turn today; see Vikunja #632). Spec-decode ON, greedy/deterministic, 64 max_new_tokens. LOCALAPPDATA redirected to a temp dir so OpenVINO recompiled cold (no warm ov-cache reuse).

not_measured: warm first-token (KV-cache / ov-cache hot) -- every tier recompiled cold under a temp LOCALAPPDATA
co-resident peak (multiple models loaded together on the shared ceiling)
multi-run variance -- n=1 per tier; these are path-verification latencies, not a throughput benchmark
AF_HYPERV (vsock) transport -- the tiers use AF_INET loopback; only the socket family differs, the mTLS code path is the production one

c1_production_round_trip.posture: production (dev_mode=False, mTLS, signed-manifest boot, real PGOV)

c3_router_in_ao_turn.result: PASS (test green; AO->router wiring absent -- see Vikunja #632)

notes: First agent-run measurements of the FULL gateway->AO chain over production mTLS -- distinct from the in-process generate_text harness (2026-06-04 entry). model_load 18.4s is consistent with the 2026-06-04 cold load (19.3s); cold load still dominates the wait. C1 first_token (3.10s) exceeds the in-process 1.50s (2026-06-04) because this is the full cross-socket production path cold; C1/C2 total_turn is short only because the benign single-sentence prompt produced a 36-char reply (latency is load+path-bound, not content-bound).
